completed_tasks.name: Fix drop-shadow rectangulation: hide img until onLoad, then fade in
Restore original chrome 3D Nissan logo (user preferred over flat black from dataset)
Replace Toyota logo with chrome 3D badge + red TOYOTA wordmark (transparent bg)

remaining_tasks.name: Fix remaining dark/invisible logos manually — user will do these one at a time
remaining_tasks.notes: Attempted bulk algorithmic fix (per-pixel dark→white conversion) but results were poor. Logos like Acura had chrome badge disappear, text became invisible. User prefers manual approach: provide custom logo files or describe fix per make.

human_actions_pending.action: Fix remaining dark logos manually, one at a time
human_actions_pending.context: Algorithmic bulk processing (dark pixel→white) did not work well. Chrome/metallic badges lost detail. Best results came from sourcing proper transparent-background logos (like Nissan chrome, Toyota chrome). User will drive which makes need fixing and may provide their own logo files.

decisions.decision: Use chrome 3D Nissan logo (pre-existing in repo) over flat black from dataset
decisions.rationale: User strongly preferred the chrome version — looks far better on dark carousel with screen blend mode

decisions.decision: Use user-provided Toyota logo (chrome badge + red wordmark, transparent bg)
decisions.rationale: Better visual quality than dataset flat logo; already had transparent background

decisions.decision: Logo path uses .replace(/\s+/g, '-') to normalize make names
decisions.rationale: Dataset uses hyphens (land-rover.png) but DB stores make as 'Land Rover' with spaces
Per-pixel dark→white conversion destroyed chrome badge detail. Acura lost its silver badge, text became invisible. Manual fixes per logo is the right approach.

next_action: Ask user which make logo they want to fix next. Best approach: user sources a transparent-bg PNG (like they did for Toyota) or describes specific color change needed. Avoid bulk algorithmic processing.
context_notes: Garage carousel is complete and live at gdimension.app. All code changes (vignette, drop-shadow fix, logo path) are committed. 354 logos are deployed. Nissan and Toyota look great with chrome 3D logos. Remaining makes with dark/invisible logos (Audi, VW, Mercedes, etc.) need manual fixes — user will tackle these at their own pace.
